title: verbascum alopecuroides
intro: Verbascum alopecuroides, also known as Woolly Mullein, is a biennial plant species belonging to the mullein family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Verbascum alopecuroides include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, bronchitis, and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Verbascum alopecuroides are often infused in tea or dried to be used in tinctures, ointments, and salves for topical application.
